text: CEASEFIRE EXPIRY WATCH: Iran-US ceasefire nominally expires May 17 — Day 79. No deal in sight. Trump returns from Beijing with no binding Iran commitments from Xi. Araghchi says ceasefire is 'very shaky.' Trump on Fox News: 'I am not going to be much more patient.' Pentagon sources tell outlets new strike package options ready for presidential authorization. Strait of Hormuz Blockade Day 75.
verificationNote: Fox News confirmed Trump patience statement. Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi 'very shaky' quote. CNBC confirmed Xi summit produced no binding Iran deal.

text: ARAGHCHI DOUBTS US SERIOUSNESS: Iranian FM stated Iran received new US messages expressing willingness to continue negotiations but Tehran has 'no trust in the Americans.' Araghchi: nuclear enrichment issue 'postponed' — not being discussed in current talks. US demands Iran ship its enriched uranium out of the country; Iran refuses. Nuclear deadlock is the core sticking point preventing any deal.
verificationNote: ANI News confirmed US message to Iran. Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi nuclear deadlock statement. WION confirmed enriched uranium issue 'postponed' framing.

text: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT OUTCOME ON IRAN: Trump says Xi offered diplomatic help to end Iran war but made no binding commitments on Hormuz reopening. Both leaders agreed Strait 'must remain open' — but Iran was not party to that statement. Xi ruled out military assistance. Trump declined to ask Xi for 'favors.' China's foreign ministry: 'no point continuing the war.' Chinese vessels permitted to sail Hormuz — suggesting IRGC selective enforcement.
verificationNote: CBS News confirmed Trump-Xi joint Hormuz statement. CNBC confirmed Xi's no-military-aid position. Al Jazeera confirmed Chinese vessels permitted passage. Fox News liveblog confirmed summit details.

text: ISRAEL-LEBANON 45-DAY CEASEFIRE EXTENSION: State Dept confirmed Israel and Lebanon agreed to 45-day ceasefire extension after 'productive and positive' Washington talks May 14-15. Next political round June 2-3. Security/military track launches Pentagon May 29. Delegation: Lebanon led by Simon Karam, Israel by Ron Dermer. Hezbollah disarmament remains unresolved — IDF still striking Hezbollah sites despite extension. Netanyahu confirmed troops remain in southern Lebanon.
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour confirmed State Dept announcement. Haaretz liveblog confirmed delegation names and June 2-3 next round. Time magazine confirmed Netanyahu troop presence statement.

text: TRUMP SOFTENS ON NUCLEAR DEMAND: On Air Force One departing Beijing, Trump signaled willingness to accept 20-year Iranian nuclear suspension (vs. permanent ban previously demanded) if it's 'real.' Also weighing lifting sanctions on Chinese companies buying Iranian oil. Congressional pushback: 52 senators + 177 House members sent letter demanding Trump reject any deal permitting enrichment. Policy shift could unlock talks — or face domestic veto.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed Trump 20-year suspension statement from Air Force One. CNBC also confirmed Chinese oil sanctions consideration. Congressional letter confirmed by multiple outlets.

text: MOJTABA KHAMENEI DEFIANCE SIGNAL: Iranian state TV broadcast statement attributed to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei: 'The only place for the United States in the Persian Gulf is at the bottom of its waters.' Still no public appearance — 75+ days since appointment. US intelligence assessed Khamenei is 'mentally sharp but isolated.' Pezeshkian claims 2.5-hour private meeting. IRGC military council reportedly blocking presidential access to Supreme Leader.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Khamenei statement via state media. CNN reported US intel assessment on May 8. Iran International reported IRGC blocking access claim. No independent verification of Khamenei's condition.

text: CONFIRMED KILL: IDF confirms elimination of Hamas Gaza chief and military wing commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Rimal neighborhood strike. 13 munitions used. IDF describes him as 'the last architect of October 7.' His family confirmed death to AP. Gaza ceasefire prospects now uncertain — Haddad was central Hamas negotiator.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed on May 16. Times of Israel and AP independently confirmed via family.

text: HORMUZ MARITIME INCIDENTS x2: UKMTO confirmed a ship seized northeast of Fujairah and diverted toward Iranian waters. Separately, Indian-flagged cargo vessel Haji Ali attacked and sunk off Oman — 14 crew rescued by Omani coast guard. Iran has now seized or sunk vessels on 3 consecutive weeks. Blockade Day 75 with no diplomatic resolution in sight.
verificationNote: UKMTO confirmed seizure. PBS NewsHour and NBC News confirmed both incidents on May 15, 2026.

text: NUCLEAR DEADLOCK HARDENS: Iran FM Araghchi told BRICS summit in New Delhi that Iran has 'no trust' in the US, calling lack of trust 'the main obstacle to any diplomatic effort.' Enriched uranium status remains a formal 'deadlock.' Meanwhile Trump offers 20-year suspension (down from permanent ban demand) — Iran has not responded. Both sides now publicly disagree on whether ceasefire even expired.
verificationNote: Araghchi statements confirmed by Al Jazeera and Washington Post. Trump offer confirmed by Times of Israel and CNBC.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED but IDF STRUCK TYRE: While Lebanon and Israel extended ceasefire 45 days in Washington talks, IDF simultaneously struck 40+ Hezbollah sites in Tyre district — 37 wounded including hospital staff and children. Israel explicitly states troops will REMAIN in southern Lebanon during extension. Next security track May 29; next talks June 2-3.
verificationNote: Lebanon ceasefire extension confirmed by PBS NewsHour/State Dept. Tyre strikes confirmed by Al Arabiya and RNZ.

text: INDIA RAISES FUEL PRICES FIRST TIME SINCE WAR: India hiked fuel prices 3 rupees/liter on May 15 as ~50% of crude supply disrupted by Hormuz blockade. Gasoline now 97.77 rupees/liter ($1.02). State refiners say 15-20 more rupees/liter needed to break even. UAE simultaneously announced accelerated West-East bypass pipeline — operational by 2027. Saudi rerouting via East-West pipeline to Yanbu. Gulf energy infrastructure adapting to new Hormuz-less reality.
verificationNote: India fuel hike confirmed by Al Jazeera and Bloomberg. UAE pipeline announcement confirmed by CNBC and Al Jazeera.

text: TRUMP-XI IRAN OUTCOMES: Joint statement agreed Hormuz 'must remain open' and Iran 'can never have nuclear weapon.' But MSNBC/Bloomberg: two sides 'disagree on what they agreed on.' Xi offered diplomatic help, ruled out military assistance to Iran. Trump says he 'didn't ask China for favors.' Araghchi: 'US contradictions deepen mistrust.' Net result: ceasefire expiry imminent with no breakthrough.
verificationNote: Trump-Xi joint statement confirmed by CNBC, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg. Chinese/US divergence noted by Bloomberg and Time magazine.

text: IRAN PGSA TOLL MECHANISM CONFIRMED: Iranian parliament security commission chair Azizi announced the imminent unveiling of the Persian Gulf Strait Authority — a formal body to control and charge for Hormuz transit. Vessels from cooperating parties pay fees; US Project Freedom escorts excluded. This is a significant escalation: Iran is now formally institutionalizing Hormuz control as a permanent revenue source, not just a temporary war measure.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 16, PressTV May 16, Times of Israel liveblog, Investing.com.

text: NUCLEAR PROPOSAL DISPUTE: Trump says US sent Iran a written nuclear deal proposal and warned of consequences without swift progress. Iran FM Araghchi directly contradicted this, saying no written proposal was received. Khamenei advisor Shamkhani separately said Iran is ready to sign IF all sanctions lifted immediately, but called US control demands a fantasy. The public contradiction over whether a formal channel exists is itself a diplomatic red flag.
verificationNote: Trump statement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. Araghchi denial confirmed. Shamkhani response confirmed by IBTimes UK and Wikipedia negotiations article.

text: OIL MARKETS DAY 78 PM: Brent closed around 09.26 (+8.1% for the week), WTI 05.42 (+11% weekly). The IEA issued a stark warning that the global oil market will remain materially undersupplied through October 2026 even if the conflict ends next month. The PGSA announcement is being interpreted by traders as Iran formally asserting permanent sovereignty over Hormuz tolls, which could structurally embed a risk premium for years. DHL forecasts 4-6 months to shipping normalization.
verificationNote: Oil prices confirmed by TradingEconomics and oilprice.com. IEA warning confirmed via Bloomberg. DHL normalization forecast confirmed via Carra Globe.

text: PAKISTAN MEDIATION LAST-DITCH EFFORT: Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived in Tehran for a two-day visit to try to salvage the collapsing US-Iran diplomatic track. Iran formally confirmed readiness for further US talks (Baghaei: proposals are reasonable). The mediator role is under scrutiny after some Trump officials accused Pakistan of softening Iran positions to Washington. Meanwhile Wikipedia consolidated casualty data now shows Israel: 49 killed / 8,609 injured and Lebanon: 2,882 killed / 8,768 wounded as of May 12.
verificationNote: Pakistan FM visit confirmed by Al Jazeera. Casualty figures confirmed by Wikipedia Casualties of the 2026 Iran war article (as of May 12, 2026).

text: IRAN THREATENS WEAPONS-GRADE ENRICHMENT IF ATTACKED AGAIN: Iranian parliament spokesman Rezaei warned May 12 that Iran could enrich uranium to 90% (weapons-grade) if the US resumes strikes. Iran holds ~400-460 kg enriched to 60% — upgradeable to weapons-grade within 1-2 weeks per Witkoff. Meanwhile Netanyahu told CBS 60 Minutes the war is 'not over' as long as enriched uranium remains in Iran, calling for physical removal. The enrichment question is the central deadlock: Iran says it is non-negotiable; US/Israel say 90% enrichment would be a red line for resumed strikes.
verificationNote: Weapons-grade enrichment threat confirmed by Euronews May 12, Times of Israel, Al Arabiya, Daily Sabah. Netanyahu 60 Minutes interview confirmed by Fortune, Jewish Insider, Bloomberg (May 10-11).

text: IDF KIA — CAPT. MAOZ RECANATI: IDF confirmed death of Capt. Maoz Israel Recanati (24, Golani Brigade 12th Battalion, from Itamar) killed by Hezbollah fiber-optic FPV drone in southern Lebanon May 16. He is the 7th IDF soldier killed since the April 17 Lebanon ceasefire and the 20th on the Lebanon front total. Six additional soldiers were wounded. Fiber-optic guided drones cannot be jammed — signal travels through physical cable. IDF admitted no effective countermeasure exists.
verificationNote: IDF named and confirmed Recanati. Times of Israel liveblog, JNS, Haaretz all confirmed the death with unit and hometown. Washington Post confirmed fiber-optic unjammable drone technology May 13.

text: IRAN RESTORES 30/33 HORMUZ MISSILE SITES: ISW (Institute for the Study of War) assessment: Iran has restored operational status to 30 of its 33 Strait of Hormuz missile launch sites — sites struck during Operation Epic Fury. IRGC Mohammad Rasoul Ollah Unit conducted combat readiness exercises in Tehran Province. IRGC Navy repositioning underway. ISW: Iran 'actively preparing for resumption of hostilities.' If talks fail, the US would face reconstituted defenses.
verificationNote: Euronews confirmed ISW assessment May 13. CNN confirmed Iran readiness preparations. IRGC exercise details confirmed by Times of Israel and GlobalSecurity operational update.

text: HOUTHIS THREATEN BAB AL-MANDAB CLOSURE: Houthis (Ansar Allah) threatened to close Bab al-Mandab Strait — linking Red Sea to Gulf of Aden — if conflict against Iran escalates or Gulf Arab states join the war. A dual closure of Hormuz AND Bab al-Mandab would cut off virtually all Gulf oil exports, threatening ~35% of global seaborne oil trade. Traffic at Bab al-Mandab already 'sharply reduced' due to threat posture and insurance withdrawal.
verificationNote: Gulf News confirmed Houthi Bab al-Mandab threat. gCaptain confirmed Houthi statements. No verified closure yet — classified as threat.

text: USS GERALD R. FORD RETURNS HOME: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78, world's largest carrier) docked at Naval Station Norfolk after a record 11-month deployment — the longest US carrier deployment since Vietnam. The carrier's strike group flew wave after wave of strikes against Iran during Operation Epic Fury. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth attended the homecoming ceremony. 5,000 sailors aboard. The Ford completed 320+ days at sea.
verificationNote: CNN confirmed Ford homecoming and Hegseth attendance May 16. Washington Post confirmed record deployment length. Navy Times confirmed 11-month deployment and homecoming date.

text: TRUMP 'TWO DAYS' THREAT: Trump told reporters (per Haaretz/Fox News May 16) the US could destroy Iran's entire infrastructure 'in two days' if he chose to — his most direct threat since the April ceasefire. This comes as the ceasefire formally expired (Iran disputes this), talks are deadlocked on enrichment duration, and Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi is in Tehran mediating. Trump is maintaining maximum pressure even as his nuclear demand softened from permanent to 20-year suspension.
verificationNote: Haaretz May 16 liveblog confirmed Trump 'two days' infrastructure destruction claim. Fox News confirmed the statement. Times of Israel liveblog also reported the threat.
